title: centaurea ispahanica subsp. ispahanica
intro: Centaurea ispahanica subsp. ispahanica, also known as the Ispahan cornflower, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and the Caucasus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Centaurea ispahanica subsp. ispahanica include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, reduce inflammation and pain, and improve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are made by drying and infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall health.
